{"query":"Spain block US sanctions ICC compliance EU response Israel Hamas conflict","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1228,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/world/spain-asks-european-commission-block-us-sanctions-icc-2026-05-06/","title":"Spain asks European Commission to block US sanctions on ICC | Reuters","content":"The EU's blocking statute is ​a ​legal ⁠mechanism designed to protect ​Europeans from ​the ⁠extraterritorial effects of sanctions imposed ⁠by ​third countries."},{"url":"https://www.timesofisrael.com/liveblog_entry/spain-asks-european-commission-to-block-us-sanctions-on-icc/","title":"Spain asks European Commission to block US sanctions on ICC | The Times of Israel","content":"The war with Iran has been draining for all of us in Israel."},{"url":"https://www.commondreams.org/news/sanchez-icc-sanctions","title":"'Spain Does Not Look the Other Way': Sánchez Calls on EU to Block Israel-US Sanctions on ICC | Common Dreams","content":"Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez asked the European Commission on Wednesday to block compliance with US sanctions against the International Criminal Court over its arrest warrants against Israeli leaders accused of war crimes and crimes against humanity in Gaza.
Last February, US President Donald Trump signed an executive order sanctioning the ICC, citing its warrants in November 2024 for the arrest of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and his former defense minister, Yoav Gallant.
"That is why, today, we ask the commission to activate the Blocking Statute, to protect the independence of the International Criminal Court and the United Nations, and their actions to end the genocide in Gaza.""},{"url":"https://www.middleeasteye.net/news/spains-sanchez-asks-eu-block-us-sanctions-icc","title":"Spain's Sanchez asks EU to block US sanctions on ICC | Middle East Eye","content":"Spain's Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez (L) looks at European Commission President Ursula Von der Leyen prior to a roundtable as part of the EU Western Balkans summit in Brussels on 17 December 2025 (Nicolas Tucat/AFP)
Since February last year, the administration of US President Donald Trump has imposed financial and visa sanctions on 11 ICC officials and the UN special rapporteur on Palestine in connection with the ICC’s arrest warrants against Israeli leaders and its ongoing Afghanistan investigation.
It primarily focuses on shielding EU operators from certain US sanctions considered to have extraterritorial reach, like those against Cuba and Iran.
MEE has contacted the Commission for comment.
Middle East Eye revealed in December that the Trump administration had unsuccessfully pressured the ICC to drop its investigations into alleged war crimes in Palestine and Afghanistan as a condition for lifting the sanctions.
She told MEE in an interview in December that the sanctions have barred her from using her credit card or any banking system that uses US dollars.
More about MEE can be found here."},{"url":"https://www.middleeastmonitor.com/20260508-understanding-the-eu-blocking-statute-spains-legal-strategy-to-shield-the-icc-from-u-s-sanctions/","title":"Understanding the EU Blocking Statute: Spain’s legal strategy to shield the ICC from US sanctions – Middle East Monitor","content":"on 6 May 2026, Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s called for the European Union to activate its Blocking Statute in response to U.S. sanctions against the International Criminal Court (ICC).
Slovenia has joined the call of Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez urging the European Union to shield the ICC from sanctions, uphold the rule of law, and strengthen the global system of justice in response to grave crimes.
More specifically, a blocking statute prohibits individuals, corporations, public and private institutions, as well as NGOs within the jurisdiction of the enacting state from adhering to or giving effect to foreign sanctions targeting the ICC.

Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez on Wednesday called on the European Commission to activate the EU's Blocking Statute to shield the International Criminal Court (ICC) and the United Nations from US sanctions. Since February last year, the administration of US President Donald Trump has imposed financial and visa sanctions on 11 ICC officials and the UN special rapporteur on Palestine in connection with the ICC’s arrest warrants against Israeli leaders and its ongoing Afghanistan investigation. The EU Blocking Statute is a regulation that aims to protect EU companies and individuals from the effects of extraterritorial sanctions imposed by third countries, essentially preventing them from complying with foreign laws that could harm their business operations within the EU, even if those laws target activities outside the EU jurisdiction.","score":0.79041743,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.timesofisrael.com/liveblog_entry/spain-asks-european-commission-to-block-us-sanctions-on-icc","title":"Spain asks European Commission to block US sanctions ...","content":"Spain has asked the European Commission to activate its blocking statute to prevent compliance with US sanctions on the International Criminal","score":0.7348247,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.commondreams.org/news/sanchez-icc-sanctions","title":"Sánchez Calls on EU to Block Israel-US Sanctions on ICC","content":"Pedro Sánchez asked the European Commission on Wednesday to block compliance with US sanctions against the International Criminal Court over","score":0.70944256,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://english.almayadeen.net/news/politics/spain-urges-eu-to-block-us-sanctions-on-icc-over-gaza-genoci","title":"Spain urges EU to block US sanctions on ICC over Gaza ...","content":"The Spanish prime minister has asked the European Commission to activate the EU's blocking statute to protect the International Criminal Court","score":0.7023877,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/world/spain-asks-european-commission-block-us-sanctions-icc-2026-05-06","title":"Spain asks European Commission to block US sanctions ...","content":"Spain has asked the European Commission to activate its blocking statute to prevent compliance with U.S. sanctions.
